response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum bar on paper — a technical bachelor's degree and roughly 20 years of hands-on software experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems — but the profile is that of a strong senior/staff product engineer, not a director-level AI strategist. There is no recognized AI expertise, no advanced AI degree, no shipped AI products or platforms of note, and no track record of engineering leadership, customer advisory, or strategy work at the scale this role demands. The recent MCP/agentic-tooling work and the GISCollective founder experience are genuine positives, but they are too recent and too small-scale to close the gap to a Principal Engineer on AI strategy.
strengths: Roughly 20 years of broad, hands-on engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency high-scale backend systems
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform used in production by Green Map
Recent practical AI-agent infrastructure work (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling) plus a history of teaching and public talks
concerns: No director/principal-level leadership or strategy experience; career has been senior IC delivery roles, not org-level technical influence
Lacks recognized AI expertise, an advanced AI-related degree, or demonstrated AI products/platforms — the core preferred qualifications
Cloud expertise is application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deploys) rather than cloud platform strategy, and external visibility is community-scale rather than industry-scale

key_factors.factor: Missing recognized AI expertise and AI product track record
Long, broad hands-on engineering experience with founder/startup background
